methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 45.3x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
SpaceForest, founded by Rafał Przybyła, is a public-stage Space & Aerospace company that has raised capital via IPO and ESA funding. The valuation universe comprises 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private comparables including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace—a total of 13 datapoints across the sector.

Two standard triangulation methods prove inapplicable to this analysis. Peer-median benchmarking fails due to insufficient narrow-stage peers: while 23 same-sector companies exist, zero qualify as same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier comparables; a sector-wide median would introduce unacceptable noise. Public-comp anchoring, the second method, requires target revenue data unavailable on file—a common constraint for private companies. The public Space & Aerospace median P/S multiple is 45.3x across the 6-ticker universe, but cannot be deployed without SpaceForest's top-line figures.

This analysis cannot produce a triangulated valuation range without either SpaceForest's revenue figures or a sufficiently dense cluster of private comparables at matching stage and scale. Key uncertainties blocking further work include: (1) SpaceForest's FY revenue and growth trajectory, (2) margin profile relative to public peers, and (3) which of the 8 private peers most closely mirror its business model and funding profile. Resolution of these inputs is prerequisite to substantive valuation work.
